project.name: Clawvato
project.description: Business operating system for solo technical founders — memory-powered AI agent across Slack, email, calendar, Drive, meetings, and Claude Code

1.name: Build the Agent
1.description: Reactive agent that responds when asked. Slack, memory, Google, Fireflies, sweeps, tasks.

2.name: Build the Brain
2.description: Smart memory plugin: embeddings, hybrid search, reranking, dedup, clustering, journaling, reflection. Agent-agnostic.

3.name: Build the Nervous System
3.description: Agent-side scheduler rebuild, webhook ingest, real-time triggers. The agent acts on its own.

4.name: Build the Judgment
4.description: Autonomous workflows — CRM, project tracking, proactive surfacing. Good autonomous decisions.

E.name: Proactive Intelligence
E.notes: Sweep pipeline working. Evolving into Phase 3 (agent scheduler + ingest).

F.name: Security Hardening
F.notes: Training wheels removed. Pre-tool security checks + output sanitization remain.

G.name: GitHub + Self-Development
G.notes: Designed (three-gate safety model). Deferred until Phase 3/4 stable.

H.name: Smart Plugin Brain
H.notes: Full intelligence migrated: embeddings, hybrid search, reranking, dedup, entity-hop, clustering, scheduler, journaling, reflection.

J.name: Hybrid Architecture
J.notes: Replaced by CC-Native Engine. Hybrid code preserved on main as fallback.

L.name: Autonomous Task Queue
L.notes: Task CRUD + channel manager complete. Agent-side scheduler needs rebuild.

O.name: CC-Native Engine
O.notes: Claude Code as core engine via Channels. Double down on CC-native.

P.name: Smart Plugin Migration
P.notes: 5-phase migration: embeddings → hybrid search + reranking → write-time dedup → entity-hop + clustering → memory scheduler + journaling. Design: docs/DESIGN_SMART_PLUGIN_MIGRATION.md

infrastructure.local: macOS, .mcp.json runs plugin as local stdio process with Railway DB URL
infrastructure.database: Railway managed Postgres (automatic backups, pgvector, tsvector)
infrastructure.deployMethod: GitHub auto-deploy — clawvato from cc-native-engine branch, clawvato-memory from main branch
infrastructure.maxPlan: Claude Max subscription — CC-native engine costs $0
infrastructure.memoryPlugin: github.com/andrewcovato/clawvato-memory — smart brain on Railway. Public: clawvato-memory-production.up.railway.app/mcp + /ingest + /health
infrastructure.memoryPluginAuth: Bearer token via MCP_AUTH_TOKEN env var

currentSprint.name: Real-Time Ingest — Pub/Sub Webhooks
goals: Gmail webhook via Google Pub/Sub (Phase A)
Deploy sidecar as separate Railway service
Reduce sweep frequency once webhooks proven (Phase C)

S10.name: Memory Excellence
workstreams: WS1: Memory scoping — soft signals (domain 1.3x, surface 1.1x boost)
WS2: Write-time dedup — ADD/UPDATE/NOOP/DELETE via Haiku
WS3: Embedding upgrade — nomic-embed-text-v1.5 (Matryoshka 384d)
WS4: Cross-encoder reranking — local ms-marco-MiniLM ($0, 48x faster)
WS5: Memory instructions — comprehensive prompt + XML-tagged context
Bonus: Entity-hop traversal, domain taxonomy, scoping refactor

S24.name: Smart Plugin Migration
workstreams: Phase 0: Module restructure (798→142 line entrypoint)
Phase 1: Embeddings at write time (nomic-embed-text-v1.5)
Phase 2: Hybrid search + RRF + cross-encoder reranking
Phase 3: Three-tier write-time dedup (heuristic → cross-encoder → Haiku)
Phase 4: Entity-hop traversal in retrieve pipeline
Phase 5: Memory scheduler (consolidation, reflection, decay)
Conversation journaling (ingest_conversation + /ingest + PostToolUse hook)
HDBSCAN semantic clustering (10 clusters discovered, auto-labeled)
Cluster expansion in retrieval pipeline
Agent wiring (retire agent-side memory stack in production)
Merged cc-native-engine → main (43 commits)

S25.name: Nervous System — Sidecar Rebuild + Task UX
workstreams: Journal hook wiring (PostToolUse) + insights scratch pad + brief auto-update on flush
Sonnet extraction upgrade (Haiku→Sonnet) + tighter prompt (quality over volume)
Plugin sync command — auto-update scripts on SessionStart across all projects
Comprehensive plugin README (install, update, architecture, hooks, tools)
Sidecar rebuild: tiered sweeps (Slack+FF 1h, Drive 6h, backfill 24h) running in-process
Task UX overhaul: deleted pins/approval/reconciliation (-685 lines), event feed (+311 lines)
Simplified task tools (4 tools, DB-only, no Slack side effects)
Sweep verified: 20 new memories within 30 min of deploy, 227 channels discovered
Pub/Sub webhook spec: docs/DESIGN_PUBSUB_WEBHOOKS.md

S27.name: Autonomous Workflows
goals: First autonomous workflows created as ad-hoc tasks
Validation: system surfaces things owner didn't ask about
S27.notes: Louvain community detection and relationship extraction moved here from S25

keyDecisions: Insights scratch pad (/tmp/clawvato-insights.md) — agent writes insights, journal hook picks up on flush
Brief auto-update on flush — mechanical guarantee, not prompt compliance
Sonnet replaces Haiku for /ingest extraction (quality > volume, ~$0.03/session)
Plugin sync command replaces check-version.sh (actually updates, not just warns)
Sidecar rebuilt: tiered sweeps in-process (no CC needed), event feed (no pins)
Deleted channel-manager.ts + approval.ts (-685 lines) — pins replaced by event feed
Task approval via natural language, not Slack reactions
Sweeps are now categorized: brain maintenance (plugin) vs data collection (sidecar) vs user tasks (CC)
Sweep:all-sources task cancelled — sweeps owned by sidecar timers
Agent-side task scheduler stays agent-side (NOT in plugin) — two schedulers by design
Pub/Sub webhook spec completed (S26): Gmail Pub/Sub + Calendar events.watch()

immediateBacklog.title: Gmail webhook via Google Pub/Sub (S26 Phase A)
Calendar webhook via events.watch() (S26 Phase B)
Deploy sidecar as separate Railway service with public URL
Louvain community detection on entity co-occurrence graph (S27)

backlog.title: Performance optimization pass (connection pooling, batch operations, MCP overhead)
Track G Phase 1: read-only GitHub access

thingsNotToRedo: DON'T REBUILD WHAT COMES NATIVELY WITH CLAUDE CODE
Don't hardcode prompts in TypeScript — all prompts in config/prompts/*.md
Don't hardcode tunables in TypeScript — all operational values in config/default.json
Always take the pain now — build for durability, no short-term hacks
Always build for the future — adopt Anthropic tech as it ships
Bug reports → investigate + plan + confirm before coding
Plan before coding — don't jump straight to implementation
Don't build bespoke tools/tables for specific entity types — unified memory handles it
Store what+why, never how — implementation details don't belong in memory
Plugin schema must exactly match Railway DB — test against real DB, not assumptions
Always use soft-delete (valid_until = NOW()) for memories, never hard DELETE
Hard DELETE can dangle superseded_by FK references
CC instances won't know about MCP tools unless system prompt teaches them
Memory intelligence belongs in the plugin, not the agent — the brain is agent-agnostic
postgres.js tagged templates silently drop ::vector cast on INSERT — use INSERT then UPDATE for embedding
Haiku max output 8192 tokens — use Sonnet for extraction
MCP config type must be 'http' not 'url' — CC schema validation rejects 'url'
MCP SDK Server.connect() is 1:1 — for HTTP concurrency, create new Server+Transport per request
Don't impose artificial caps on query results — sensible defaults, no hard maximums
CC session-scoped scheduling (/loop, CronCreate) dies on restart — own the scheduler
Two schedulers: memory (plugin, self-maintenance) and agent (user-facing tasks, needs Slack/Google)
Tasks and workflows are data, not code — new capabilities shouldn't require a deploy
Journal hook uses /ingest REST endpoint (not MCP) — simpler for fire-and-forget background calls
HDBSCAN minClusterSize scales with corpus: max(3, n/50)
Local .mcp.json runs plugin as stdio process — MCP calls go to local Node, not Railway
Always bump clawvato-memory version on push — GitHub Packages won't publish duplicate versions
Insights scratch pad (/tmp/clawvato-insights.md) — agent writes, journal hook picks up. Never use store_fact for routine insights.
Brief auto-update is mechanically guaranteed via journal flush — don't depend on prompt compliance for cross-surface awareness
Sonnet > Haiku for /ingest extraction — Haiku extracts noise (file paths, tool usage), Sonnet has judgment to skip derivable facts
Three categories of scheduled work: brain maintenance (plugin), data collection (sidecar), user tasks (CC via Slack)
Sweeps: high-water marks + plugin dedup = two-layer idempotency. Safe to increase frequency, never duplicates.
Best refactors delete more than they add — -685/+311 lines for the task UX overhaul
